title: Resonance torpedo

A '''resonance torpedo''' was a [[Missile/Legends|missile]] that made a [[Star/Legends|star]] go [[Supernova/Legends|supernova]] by causing a chain reaction in the core. It was developed at the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] hidden [[Maw Installation]] and was the primary weapon of the [[Sun Crusher]].<ref name="TCSWE">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> These torpedoes, in their prototype form, were used during the [[Battle of the Sixela system]] to destroy the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Rebel Alliance starfleet]]. [[Standard year/Legends|Years]] later, rogue [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Kyp Durron]] stole the Sun Crusher and used its torpedoes in a rampage against the Empire.

==Description==
{{Quote|With any prototype weapon of this sort, there is a one in three hundred twenty-seven chance that the weapon will fail.|[[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] in regards to the torpedoes during the Battle of the Sixela system|<ref name="RF: Uprising">''[[Rebel Force: Uprising]]''</ref>}}
Resonance torpedoes were activated by passing through a [[resonance energizer]], and were then launched through the [[resonance projector]].<ref name="EGWT">''[[The Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology]]''</ref> The resonance torpedo was purple and yellow when inactive, but when fired, it took the form of a greenish-blue ellipsoid moving at subspace.<ref name="Jedi Search">''[[Jedi Search]]''</ref>

[[File:Resonancetorpedo.jpg|250px|thumb|right|Resonance torpedo schematic]]
Unlike [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]]-based superweapons, resonance torpedoes, upon detonation, left behind organic remains, including the frozen blood of any inhabitants of the planet destroyed by it. This could be especially seen with the Caridan Nebula created in the aftermath of Kyp Durron's destruction of Carida.

==History==
Resonance torpedoes were conceptualized during the development of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] between [[19 BBY/Legends|19]] and [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], as a proposed weapons system for the immense battle station. These variants were significantly larger than the final version.

Resonance torpedoes were developed around [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]] at the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] hidden [[Maw Installation]]. Rogue Imperial [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Rezi Soresh]] used his knowledge of the hidden facility to acquire three of the prototype weapons. After luring those responsible for his exile, the Imperial leader [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], and the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Rebel Alliance starfleet]] to his outpost in the [[Sixela system/Legends|Sixela system]], Soresh launched three torpedoes at the system's star. The torpedoes did not travel at the speed of light, taking nearly three hours to start the chain reaction. The star went supernova, turning the system into a storm of [[Radiation/Legends|radiation]] and debris. The fleets escaped before the chain reaction, and Soresh was [[Death/Legends|killed]] by [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]].<ref name="RF: Uprising" />

The [[Sun Crusher]] had eleven of these torpedoes. Seven were fired into the [[Cauldron Nebula/Legends|Cauldron Nebula]] to ignite a cluster of hot blue suns to destroy the [[Basilisk (Star Destroyer)|''Basilisk'']] and ''[[Gorgon]]''. The ''Basilisk'' was destroyed due to being already damaged, but ''Gorgon'' escaped with reserve [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] and a seared hull. One was used to destroy the [[Carida system/Legends|Carida system]] while another was used to ignite a red dwarf and destroy a shipyard for [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyers]].<ref name="Jedi Search" />

The final two were used in attempts to destroy, or at least disable, the [[Death Star prototype]] at the [[Battle of the Maw]] Installation. They were never seen again after the Sun Crusher followed the prototype Death Star into one of [[Maw Cluster/Legends|The Maw's]] black holes.<ref name="Champions of the Force">''[[Champions of the Force]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Resonance torpedoes first appeared in [[Star Wars: The Jedi Academy Trilogy|The Jedi Academy Trilogy]].
